lede: A look at February 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ronald Reagan and Chongzhen Emperor, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Stephen Harper
events.desc: Stephen Harper becomes Prime Minister of Canada.

events.title: Mary Gaudron
events.desc: Justice Mary Gaudron becomes the first woman to be appointed to the High Court of Australia.

events.title: Manchester United F.C.
events.desc: Eight Manchester United F.C. players and 15 other passengers are killed in the Munich air disaster.

events.title: Elizabeth II
events.desc: Elizabeth II becomes Queen of the United Kingdom and her other Realms and Territories and Head of the Commonwealth upon the death of her father, George VI. At the exact moment of succession, she was in a tree house at the Treetops Hotel in Kenya.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Great Raids Against Helsinki begins.

events.title: Washington Naval Treaty
events.desc: The Washington Naval Treaty is signed in Washington, D.C., limiting the naval armaments of United States, Britain, Japan, France, and Italy.

events.title: Municipalities of Finland
events.desc: The municipal administration of Finland is established.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Forces under the command of Ulysses S. Grant and Andrew H. Foote give the Union its first victory of the war, capturing Fort Henry, Tennessee in the Battle of Fort Henry.

events.title: Massachusetts
events.desc: Massachusetts becomes the sixth state to ratify the United States Constitution.

events.title: New York (state)
events.desc: New York became the third state to ratify the Articles of Confederation.

births.name: Ronald Reagan
births.line: American actor and politician, 40th President of the United States

deaths.text: Jimmy Van Heusen, American pianist and composer
Leo von Caprivi, German general and politician, chancellor of Germany

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mateo Correa Magallanes (one of Saints of the Cristero War)
Christian feast day: Paul Miki and Twenty-six Martyrs of Japan
